prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_svr', seasonality_champion='DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9951) and high seasonality variance (0.7864), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_rf", "late": "CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["catboost", "DWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9951,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9951) and high seasonality variance (0.7864), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"shrinkage": 0.25, "top_k": 3}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.47) and seasonality variance (0.79). n_unique_winners=6 indicates diverse per-horizon specialists. RMSE spread ratio=0.9951 shows extreme model dispersion, favoring robust combination methods. With only 3 windows, small top_k=3 prevents overfitting. dba_combination handles phase-shifted peaks while inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.25 provides adaptive weighting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific strengths."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). All 4 types are represented. No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no single candidate dominates all metrics (best_single_by_validation leads SMAPE, robust_median leads MAPE/RMSE/POCID). With n_windows=3 and diverse type distribution, current set is optimal. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["robust_median", "trimmed_mean_r0.30", "best_single_by_validation"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has only 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.47). Universe shows robust_median (RMSE=773) and trimmed_mean_r0.30 (RMSE=777) significantly outperform current best (dba_combination RMSE=849). n_unique_winners=6 favors per-horizon selection - adding best_single_by_validation for SMAPE improvement (universe SMAPE=0.246 vs current 0.274). Current set lacks robustness methods; adding these addresses stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.41).",
  "changes": ["added robust_median for RMSE stability", "added trimmed_mean_r0.30 for SMAPE improvement", "added best_single_by_validation for per-horizon diversity"],
  "when_good": "n_windows >= 6 and relative_spread_mean < 0.25"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9951) and high seasonality variance (0.7864), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.47) and seasonality variance (0.79). n_unique_winners=6 indicates diverse per-horizon specialists. RMSE spread ratio=0.9951 shows extreme model dispersion, favoring robust combination methods. With only 3 windows, small top_k=3 prevents overfitting. dba_combination handles phase-shifted peaks while inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.25 provides adaptive weighting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific strengths.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). All 4 types are represented. No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no single candidate dominates all metrics (best_single_by_validation leads SMAPE, robust_median leads MAPE/RMSE/POCID). With n_windows=3 and diverse type distribution, current set is optimal. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 4 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.47). Universe shows robust_median (RMSE=773) and trimmed_mean_r0.30 (RMSE=777) significantly outperform current best (dba_combination RMSE=849). n_unique_winners=6 favors per-horizon selection - adding best_single_by_validation for SMAPE improvement (universe SMAPE=0.246 vs current 0.274). Current set lacks robustness methods; adding these addresses stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.41).
